mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when FEDFUNDS trends upward over the prior 3 months.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS vintage_value=0.54 as of 2016-12-01, near cycle lows post-2015 liftoff
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and 20d forward returns stays below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or returns following FEDFUNDS increases are not significantly more negative than baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points relative to its trailing 12-month minimum, it signals an inflection into labor market deterioration that historically precedes recessionary equity drawdowns, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises >= 0.3pp above its trailing 12-month minimum
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=4.7% as of 2016-12-01, near multi-year lows, trigger currently inactive
failure_condition: In triggered instances, SPX 60d forward returns are positive more than 55% of the time, or the Sahm-style trigger fires with no subsequent negative-return skew across two consecutive business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) indicate a broad-based consumer and residential demand contraction that jointly signals weakening aggregate demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days beyond what either series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM decline AND HOUST MoM decline simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage_value=469092 vs revised 459305, HOUST vintage=1090 vs revised 1140 as of late 2016
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS+HOUST decline signal produces forward return distributions statistically indistinguishable from either single-series decline signal (interaction term coefficient not significant at p < 0.10 in walk-forward regression).

mechanism_narrative: During mid-cycle expansions, sustained positive INDPRO growth reflects healthy industrial capacity utilization and corporate earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days; this relationship is regime-dependent and weakens or reverses in late-cycle or contraction phases where rising output can presage overheating.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage_value=103.8589 vs revised 98.3491, indicating positive industrial activity as of 2016-11-01
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth periods fail to produce 60d forward returns above the unconditional sample mean in more than half of rolling 3-year windows, or the relationship inverts sign during two consecutive expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in PAYEMS reflects ongoing labor demand strength and consumer income support, providing a persistent always-on positive signal for S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as payroll data is released and digested by markets.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage_value=145303 vs revised 145408 as of 2016-12-01, indicating continued payroll expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS MoM change and 5d forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive PAYEMS prints are not followed by above-median 5d returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST of more than 10% from its trailing 12-month peak signals a sharp deterioration in residential construction activity, a leading indicator of consumer confidence and credit conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days once the threshold is crossed.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST revised_value=1140 thousand units as of 2016-11-01, near post-crisis recovery highs
failure_condition: Triggered HOUST drawdown events are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or no statistically significant mean return difference vs. non-triggered periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A stagflation-type interaction where INDPRO growth turns negative while CPIAUCSL inflation simultaneously accelerates signals supply-side cost-push pressure without corresponding output growth, compressing corporate margins and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth < 0 AND CPIAUCSL YoY inflation rising
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage_value=242.348 vs revised 242.026 as of 2016-11-01
failure_condition: Joint INDPRO-decline/CPI-acceleration episodes fail to produce more negative 60d forward returns than either signal alone (interaction coefficient not significant at p < 0.10), or such episodes are followed by positive returns in over 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-rate, post-recession recovery regime where FEDFUNDS remains below 1%, accommodative monetary policy supports risk asset valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days, a relationship that weakens or disappears once policy normalizes above this threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.54% as of 2016-12-01, consistent with near-zero policy regime
failure_condition: 5d forward returns during FEDFUNDS < 1% periods are not significantly higher than the full-sample average (p > 0.05), or the positive skew disappears in more than half of sub-periods tested.
